# Nutrition Plan for Muscle Gain & Strength

## Daily Caloric Intake
**~2,800-3,200 calories/day** (slight surplus for muscle building)
- Your BMR is ~1,800 cal; add training + surplus

## Macronutrient Targets

| Macro | Daily Target | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| **Protein** | 170-190g | 2g per kg bodyweight |
| **Carbs** | 350-400g | Fuel for training & recovery |
| **Fat** | 85-100g | ~30% of calories |

## Key Foods to Prioritize

### Proteins
- Chicken breast, lean beef, salmon
- Eggs, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese
- Lentils, chickpeas, tofu

### Carbs
- Oats, brown rice, quinoa
- Sweet potatoes, whole grain bread
- Fruits (bananas, berries)

### Fats
- Olive oil, avocados, nuts
- Fatty fish (salmon, mackerel)
- Nut butters

## Additional Tips
✓ Eat in a **+300-500 calorie surplus**  
✓ Time carbs **around workouts**  
✓ Stay hydrated (3-4L water/day)  
✓ Eat every 3-4 hours  
✓ Consider creatine & whey protein (optional)

Would you like a sample daily meal plan?

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers a full day with breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks
Total daily protein intake is at least 1.6g per kilogram of body weight
Includes specific food items and approximate portion sizes for each meal
Emphasizes recovery-supporting nutrients (protein, carbohydrates, micronutrients)

The task explicitly requires 'a contact, concise 1 day meal plan' with 'specific food items and approximate portion sizes for each meal' - neither of which are present in the solution.

# Nutrition Plan for Performance & Endurance Maintenance

## Quick Stats
- **BMI:** 24.1 (healthy range)
- **Estimated daily calorie needs:** ~2,400-2,800 kcal (depending on activity level)

## Macronutrient Breakdown

| Macronutrient | Daily Target | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Protein** | 110-130g (1.4-1.7g/kg) | Muscle maintenance & recovery |
| **Carbs** | 300-380g (4-5g/kg) | Fuel for endurance work |
| **Fats** | 65-95g (20-25% of calories) | Hormone production & health |

## Sample Daily Eating Pattern

**Breakfast**
- Oatmeal with berries, honey, and almonds
- Greek yogurt
- Orange juice

**Mid-Morning Snack**
- Banana with peanut butter

**Lunch**
- Lean protein (chicken/fish/tofu)
- Brown rice or sweet potato
- Vegetables

**Pre/During Activity** (if needed)
- Sports drink, banana, or energy bar

**Post-Workout** (within 2 hours)
- Protein + carbs (e.g., chicken with rice)

**Dinner**
- Lean meat/fish
- Whole grain
- Vegetables with olive oil

**Evening Snack** (optional)
- Casein-rich option (cottage cheese, milk)

## Key Principles
✓ Eat whole foods 80-90% of the time  
✓ Time carbs around workouts  
✓ Stay hydrated (3-4L water daily)  
✓ Adjust calories based on training intensity

Would you like specific meal ideas or help with meal prep?

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers full 24-hour period with breakfast, lunch, dinner, and 1-2 snacks
Includes balanced macronutrients (carbs for energy, protein for recovery, healthy fats) appropriate for soccer training
All meals and snacks are quick to prepare or consume
Total plan fits on one page or concise format

The solution reads more like a template or framework than a concrete 1-day meal plan with specific portions and timing.

# Marathon Training Nutrition Plan

## Daily Calorie Needs
- **BMR**: ~1,700 calories
- **With training**: ~2,800-3,200 calories/day
- **Carb-loading phase**: 3,200-3,600 calories/day

## Macronutrient Breakdown (Carb-Loading)

| Macro | % | Grams | Purpose |
|-------|---|-------|---------|
| **Carbs** | 65% | 520-585g | Fuel for endurance |
| **Protein** | 15% | 120-135g | Recovery & muscle repair |
| **Fat** | 20% | 70-80g | Energy & hormone health |

## Recommended Foods by Category

### **Primary Carbs** (Emphasize these)
- Pasta, rice, oats, bread, potatoes
- Bagels, pancakes, cereal
- Fruits: bananas, dates, berries

### **Lean Proteins**
- Chicken, turkey, fish
- Eggs, Greek yogurt
- Legumes (beans, lentils)

### **Healthy Fats**
- Olive oil, nuts, avocado
- Fatty fish (salmon, mackerel)
- Seeds

### **Hydration**
- 3-4L water daily
- Electrolyte drinks during long runs

## Sample Daily Structure
- **Breakfast**: Oatmeal + banana + honey
- **Lunch**: Pasta + grilled chicken + veggies
- **Snacks**: Rice cakes, fruit, energy bars
- **Dinner**: Rice + salmon + sweet potato

*Adjust portions based on training intensity and individual hunger cues.*

# Nutrition Plan for Weight Maintenance & Lean Muscle

## Quick Assessment
- **BMI**: 18.7 (healthy range)
- **Goal**: Preserve muscle while maintaining weight
- **Key focus**: Adequate protein + whole foods

## Daily Nutrition Targets
- **Calories**: ~1,900-2,100 kcal (maintenance)
- **Protein**: 90-115g daily (1.9-2.4g per kg body weight for muscle preservation)
- **Carbs**: 230-290g
- **Fats**: 50-65g

## Protein Sources (Vegetarian, No Nuts)
- Greek yogurt & cottage cheese
- Eggs & egg whites
- Legumes (lentils, chickpeas, beans)
- Tofu & tempeh
- Seitan
- Protein powder (pea, hemp, or whey-based)
- Paneer/dairy cheese

## Sample Daily Breakdown

**Breakfast**: Eggs + whole grain toast + berries

**Snack**: Greek yogurt + seeds

**Lunch**: Lentil curry + rice + vegetables

**Snack**: Protein smoothie (pea protein + banana)

**Dinner**: Tofu stir-fry + quinoa + vegetables

## Key Tips
✓ Distribute protein across all meals  
✓ Include strength training 3-4x/week  
✓ Prioritize whole grains, legumes, vegetables  
✓ Stay hydrated

Would you like specific meal ideas or a weekly plan?

# Nutrition Plan for Muscle Gain (Vegan, No Soy)

## Daily Calorie & Macro Targets
- **Calories**: ~2,800-3,000/day (slight surplus for muscle gain)
- **Protein**: 130-145g/day (1.8-2g per kg bodyweight)
- **Carbs**: 350-400g/day
- **Fat**: 75-90g/day

## Key Protein Sources (Without Soy)
- **Legumes**: Lentils, chickpeas, black beans, peas (combine with grains)
- **Grains**: Quinoa, buckwheat, amaranth
- **Nuts & Seeds**: Peanuts, almonds, hemp seeds, pumpkin seeds
- **Plant-Based Proteins**: Pea protein powder, rice protein, hemp protein
- **Others**: Nutritional yeast, tempeh alternatives

**Breakfast**: Oatmeal + peanut butter + banana + hemp seeds
**Lunch**: Lentil & chickpea curry with rice + vegetables
**Snack**: Hummus + nuts + fruit
**Dinner**: Quinoa + roasted chickpeas + sweet potato + broccoli
**Snack**: Protein shake (pea/rice protein + almond milk)

## Important Notes
✓ Combine incomplete proteins (grains + legumes)
✓ Track protein intake carefully
✓ Consider B12 supplement
✓ Strength training is essential for muscle gain
✓ Monitor progress and adjust calories as needed

# Nutrition Plan for Performance & Hydration

## Daily Macronutrient Targets
Based on 183cm, 78kg (likely athletic build):
- **Calories**: 2,400-2,800 kcal/day
- **Protein**: 120-156g (1.5-2g per kg)
- **Carbs**: 360-420g (4.5-5.5g per kg) — fuel for performance
- **Fats**: 65-95g (healthy range)

## Key Foods by Category

**Carbohydrates** (performance focus)
- Oats, brown rice, quinoa, sweet potatoes
- Whole grain bread, pasta
- Fruits: bananas, berries, oranges

**Protein** (recovery & muscle maintenance)
- Chicken, turkey, lean beef
- Fish (salmon for omega-3s)
- Eggs, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese
- Legumes (beans, lentils)

**Hydration**
- Water: 3-4L daily minimum
- Sports drinks (with electrolytes) during intense training
- Coconut water, herbal tea
- Limit caffeine before hydration-critical periods

**Healthy Fats**
- Olive oil, avocado
- Nuts, seeds
- Fatty fish

## Meal Timing
- **Pre-workout**: Carbs + moderate protein (1-2 hours before)
- **Post-workout**: Carbs + protein within 30-60 minutes
- **Throughout day**: Balanced meals every 3-4 hours

Would you like specifics on workout type or daily meal examples?

# Nutrition Plan for Performance & Endurance

## Daily Macros (Estimated)
- **Calories**: ~2,400-2,700 (adjust based on activity level)
- **Protein**: 130-150g (1.8-2.1g per kg)
- **Carbs**: 350-400g (4.9-5.6g per kg)
- **Fat**: 65-75g (0.9-1.0g per kg)

## Protein Sources (Lactose-Free)
- Chicken, turkey, lean beef
- Fish & salmon
- Eggs
- Legumes (lentils, chickpeas, beans)
- Tofu & tempeh
- Lactose-free protein powder

## Carbs for Endurance
- Oats, brown rice, quinoa
- Sweet potatoes, regular potatoes
- Whole grain bread/pasta
- Fruits (bananas, berries, apples)
- Honey, maple syrup

## Healthy Fats
- Olive oil, avocado oil
- Nuts & seeds (almonds, chia, flax)
- Fatty fish
- Coconut oil

## Lactose-Free Alternatives
- Fortified almond/oat milk
- Nutritional yeast
- Lactose-free yogurt (if tolerated)
- Coconut yogurt

## Sample Daily Structure
- **Breakfast**: Oats + banana + almond butter
- **Snack**: Fruit + nuts
- **Lunch**: Grilled chicken + rice + vegetables
- **Snack**: Lactose-free protein shake
- **Dinner**: Fish + sweet potato + greens

**Adjust portions based on training intensity and personal energy needs.**
